title: error: default value for packed wire port results in syntax error
body:
I'm getting a syntax error when trying to compile a file with a default value for an input vector wire port unless I declare the wire as wire logic. A non-vector port compiles but generates a warning for a procedural assignment to a net type (but no such error if it's wire logic).

I found this somewhat confusing since I was seeing errors in sims that compiled under xsim (which is admittedly permissive of many unspecified things), but after some digging in the spec I couldn't find why this wasn't syntactically valid. Also as best I can tell from the verilog spec (see the bottom of the post), `input wire` ports should infer to `input wire logic` by default 

So I would expect this to be treated as a wire logic by default (no warning for a procedural assignment  and no syntax error).

Here's a module that produces an error when linted.

```
module tricky_ports #()
(
    input wire [7:0] assigned_wire_vector = '0
    // input wire assigned_single_wire = '0 // Generates a warning but does compile successfully
);
endmodule : tricky_ports
```

I get the following error when I run using:
```
rj@ubuntu20_04:~/misc/verilator$ bin/verilator --lint-only ./single_port.sv 
%Error: ../verilog-experiments/single_port.sv:8:43: syntax error, unexpected '=', expecting ')'
    8 |     input wire [7:0] assigned_wire_vector = '0
      |                                           ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

Changing the declaration to `input wire logic [7:0] assigned_wire_vector = '0` results in a successful compilation.

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
I ran this using latest master :`Verilator 5.033 devel rev v5.032-88-g7ec32799b`
When I built this I did see some prints about bison version, but it seemed innocuous. 
My bison version is 3.5.1 and I built verilator against g++-10

What OS and distribution are you using?
Ubuntu 20.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I think it's relatively doubtful especially since the workaround of explicitly declaring wire logic solves the issue. I did look at the yak file a bit, but I've never used bison before so it would be quite outside my wheel house to root cause and solve.

Verilator spec 23.2.2.3 refers to rules for port kind/data type.
As best I can tell from my reading 
> If the data type is omitted, it shall default to logic , except for interconnect ports, which have no
data type.
> If the port kind is omitted:
> For input and inout ports, the port shall default to a net of default net type. The default net
type can be changed using the `default_nettype compiler directive

Example in this section seems to strongly suggest this should infer to wire logic and hence allow procedural assignments.
>module mh5 (input x); // input wire logic x

Overall this seems like a really minor issue with an easy workaround that's likely quite hard to fix so I'd understand it not really being worth dealing with, but I couldn't find any existing tracker that documents this behavior.

Aside: I think it's interesting that the dumped syntax tree entry for a single wire port with and without logic looks nearly identical:
`    1:2: VAR 0x555556ff4d80 <e1306> {e12aq} @dt=0x555556fe4900@(G/w1)  assigned_single_wire [PI] INPUT [P] [VSTATIC]  WIRE`
`
    1:2: VAR 0x555556ff4d80 <e1306> {e12aw} @dt=0x555556fe4900@(G/w1)  assigned_single_wire [PI] INPUT [P] [VSTATIC]  WIRE
code_blocks:
module tricky_ports #()
(
    input wire [7:0] assigned_wire_vector = '0
    // input wire assigned_single_wire = '0 // Generates a warning but does compile successfully
);
endmodule : tricky_ports
rj@ubuntu20_04:~/misc/verilator$ bin/verilator --lint-only ./single_port.sv 
%Error: ../verilog-experiments/single_port.sv:8:43: syntax error, unexpected '=', expecting ')'
    8 |     input wire [7:0] assigned_wire_vector = '0
      |                                           ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
